docstring:
/**
 * @brief Deregister this module's transactional-memory clone table.
 *
 * CRT helper (paired with register_tm_clones) that is invoked during
 * shared-object/binary teardown to undo any registration performed at
 * load time. It looks up the weakly-referenced GCC TM runtime symbols
 * and, when both are present, hands the module's clone-table address
 * (&__TMC_END__) to the runtime for removal.
 *
 * The function is a no-op when either:
 *   - the weak symbol __TMC_END__ resolves to NULL (i.e. this module
 *     has no transactional-memory clone table), or
 *   - the weak symbol _ITM_deregisterTMCloneTable resolves to NULL
 *     (i.e. no TM runtime is linked into the process).
 *
 * @note Symbols __TMC_END__ and _ITM_deregisterTMCloneTable are
 *       declared with __attribute__((weak)), so unresolved references
 *       compare equal to NULL rather than causing a link failure.
 * @note This is the standard boilerplate emitted by GCC in crtbegin;
 *       it is typically wired up as a destructor and is not meant to
 *       be called from user code.
 */
assumptions: This is the standard glibc crtstuff.c deregister_tm_clones boilerplate emitted by gcc; reconstructed from the canonical source rather than the decompiled ops.
arg0 in the pseudocode is actually the address of __TMC_LIST__ (compared against &__TMC_END__ to detect an empty TM clone table); treated as the conventional compiler-emitted comparison.
The GOT load *&[var0+0x3fe0] is the lazily-resolved weak symbol _ITM_deregisterTMCloneTable; the null check on it is the standard weak-symbol guard.
`ret(__TMC_END__)` is the indirect call through that weak symbol with &__TMC_END__ as its argument.
divergences: [low] other: The rewrite compares against NULL via a comment, while the pseudocode compares &__TMC_END__ to arg0 (the __TMC_LIST__ address passed in). This is the canonical crtstuff idiom and semantically identical for the standard boilerplate.
[low] other: Rewrite declares __TMC_END__ as void* and takes its address; pseudocode uses __TMC_END__ directly as an address. Equivalent under the standard boilerplate convention.
